commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=926f9538f501d4ca504b3a8a62e01827b7796ac9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR persists followup queues in shared SQLite, migrates legacy queue sidecars through doctor, restores queued runs at startup, and wakes restored sessions through heartbeat recovery.

PR surface: Source +1003, Tests +1413, Generated +16, Other +1. Total +2433 across 26 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main keeps followup queues process-local, so queued messages can be lost on process restart; I did not run a live gateway repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the contributor must provide current-head real Telegram proof and the branch still needs a delivery-context fix.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts, followup messages queued during an active agent turn are silently lost.

## Solution

Persist followup queues in shared SQLite (`followup_queue_entries`), migrate legacy JSON sidecars via doctor, rehydrate from live config, and wake restored routes on gateway startup so production idle-kick can drain them.

Classify `followup-queue-restore` heartbeats as wake payloads so empty/comment-only `HEARTBEAT.md` cannot skip restored-queue system events before drain.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Followup messages queued during an active Telegram turn survive gateway restart and can be delivered afterward without silent loss; restored-queue recovery heartbeats must bypass `HEARTBEAT.md` idle gates, including valid sessions whose agents are outside the routine heartbeat map.
- Real environment tested: Local machine `joseph-MS-7B51`, systemd `openclaw-gateway.service`, Telegram DM chatId `6300969793`, sessionKey `agent:main:telegram:slash:6300969793`.
- Current-head freshness update (June 15, 2026): PR head is now `926f9538f501d4ca504b3a8a62e01827b7796ac9`.
  - The branch was rebuilt cleanly on current `origin/main`, removing the unrelated provider/runtime surface that had crept into the old PR branch.
  - `9655f45d9c40a785ff1360df914c307002745cd5` keeps the restored-followup recovery wake alive for valid sessions outside the routine heartbeat map.
  - `926f9538f501d4ca504b3a8a62e01827b7796ac9` repairs the rebased queue restore paths so older persisted queue state without newer summary fields still restores and drains correctly.
- Earlier live Telegram proof remains documented from the same user-facing workflow: restored followup prompt text was read from persisted queue and delivered to Telegram after gateway restart, with journal evidence including `[followup-queue-recovery] requested heartbeat wake for 1 restored followup queue route(s)` and Telegram outbound send confirmation for Joseph's DM.
- Current-head validation now covers the rebuilt branch surface directly:
  - gateway recovery shard passed after the clean rebuild
  - heartbeat recovery scheduling shards passed on the rebuilt head
  - SQLite persistence + migration shards passed on the rebuilt head
  - queue persist / restart drain / real SQLite round-trip shards passed on the rebuilt head
- What was not re-run on the rebuilt head: a fresh final-head live Telegram mid-turn proof (message #2 queued while turn #1 is active, then gateway restart, then exactly-once recovery) has not yet been re-captured after the clean branch rebuild. The in-process round-trip and recovery scheduler coverage below do pass on the rebuilt head.

## Tests and validation

Current head `926f9538f501d4ca504b3a8a62e01827b7796ac9` (local, June 15, 2026):

```bash
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain.ts \
  src/infra/state-migrations.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/gateway/server-followup-queue-recovery.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/infra/followup-queue-sqlite.test.ts \
  src/infra/state-migrations.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue.drain-restart.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts
```

Results:
- `tsgo:core`: passed
- focused oxlint on the rebased repair surface: passed
- gateway shard: **4 files, 24 tests passed**
- heartbeat recovery shard: **2 files, 64 tests passed**
- SQLite + migration shard: **2 files, 12 tests passed**
- queue persist / restart / round-trip shard: **3 files, 38 tests passed**

## Notes

- The old dirty PR branch was preserved separately before the clean replay; the live PR branch now points at the rebuilt queue-only branch.
- The remaining merge blocker, if any, should be fresh proof/risk handling on the rebuilt head rather than the earlier conflict/unrelated-diff problems.
target.author: jewseppi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
